alerts.title: US-Iran ceasefire collapses; reciprocal strikes resume over Hormuz
alerts.summary: After tentative 28-29 June stand-down, both US and Iran launched additional strikes on 30 June. CENTCOM confirms US struck multiple Iranian targets; Iran targeted commercial shipping near Strait of Hormuz. Iranian FM Araghchi hardened diplomatic demands in Baghdad talks, insisting full Iranian control of Hormuz and exclusion of foreign military presence. Nuclear breakout window estimated 1-2 weeks. Sources: incidents (CENTCOM, PBS, NBC, BBC, AP News), global_metrics (threat level 9/10 - CRITICAL).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Iran

alerts.title: Iran's uranium enrichment at 60% U-235; breakout time narrows to 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: IAEA verification reports confirm Iran maintains 60% uranium enrichment level amid negotiations. Breakout time estimate has shortened to approximately 1-2 weeks from previous assessments. US insists no Iranian funds will be unfrozen unless nuclear progress materializes; however, both sides publicly dispute whether nuclear matters should be part of current talks framework. Source: global_metrics (nuclear headlines, IAEA verification reports), telegram (ME Spectator ~45K views flagging nuclear stalemate on 29 June).

alerts.title: Israel sustains multi-front strikes across Gaza, Lebanon, southern Syria simultaneously
alerts.summary: IDF confirmed airstrikes on southern Syria (forcing civilian evacuation), Gaza central zone (killing 8+ Palestinians including 2 children), and southern Lebanon border positions. Hezbollah publicly condemned new ceasefire arrangement despite Israeli-US de-escalation framework. Coordinated strikes indicate sustained Israeli operations across three territorial sectors independent of US-Iran pause. Sources: incidents (Al Jazeera, BBC, Reuters, Boston Herald, WGN-TV, Sentinel Colorado).

alerts.title: US maintains four-carrier forward presence with submarine contingency in Persian Gulf
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea 15.36°N, 42.27°E), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea 21.31°N, 60.22°E), USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean 33.84°N, 29.95°E), and USS Theodore Roosevelt (Pacific 15.34°N, 115.07°E) remain deployed. USS Florida submarine positioned in Persian Gulf (26.26°N, 52.23°E). Three destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) and two amphibious ships (Bataan, Boxer) maintain Red Sea/Pacific readiness. Asset configuration sustains deterrence against Iranian escalation and supports Israeli operations. Source: assets (live-osint confirmed positioning, 54 articles analyzed).
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf, Mediterranean, Pacific

alerts.title: US domestic sentiment shifts toward Trump, Supreme Court amid Iran crisis
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US positive sentiment at 56% with spike in 'trump' (73 mentions), 'court' (52), 'supreme' (50), and 'power' (20) keywords over 24 hours. Negative sentiment remains at 39%, dominated by conflict-adjacent terms ('fire' 16, 'act' 11). Shift reflects domestic political polarization during active Persian Gulf crisis rather than traditional hawkish rally-around-flag pattern. Source: sentiment feed (US region analysis).

analysis_note: US-Iran ceasefire framework has fractured within 48 hours despite initial agreement; Iran's nuclear breakout window has narrowed to 1-2 weeks; Israel continues independent multi-front operations undeterred by US-Iran pause; four-carrier US presence suggests sustained deterrence posture rather than de-escalation.
